Internship · E-VAS Telecom · May–Aug 2026 · Cairo

Industrial AI Copilot & IIoT Platform

LangChainRAG MCPn8n QdrantChromaDB Gemma EmbeddingsOpenAI text-embedding-3 OllamaPython IIoT · PLCsNumPyPandas
GitHub (add link)
Situation

Cement plants, energy facilities, and process-industry sites generate enormous volumes of sensor data — temperature, vibration, pressure, energy consumption — yet operators relied on periodic manual inspections and reactive maintenance cycles. Equipment failures were causing unplanned downtime with no unified intelligence layer to surface predictive signals from the noise.

Task

As the AI & IIoT Engineer Intern, I owned the full stack — from PLC and sensor connectivity at the factory floor to AI-driven analytics and an operator-facing copilot — with the mandate to make it production-ready for real industrial clients, not just a proof of concept.

Action

Built production RAG pipelines using LangChain — embedding proprietary maintenance manuals and fault logs with Gemma and OpenAI embedding models, storing vectors in Qdrant and ChromaDB, and serving grounded operator responses through a retrieval + re-ranking chain. Layered MCP agent pipelines on top, giving LLMs structured tool access to live sensor streams and operational databases. Deployed local LLMs via Ollama for air-gapped factory environments. Automated the full data flow — acquisition from PLCs and sensors through to AI inference and alerting — using n8n orchestration workflows. Tied everything together in equipment health dashboards covering KPIs, fault detection, and predictive maintenance signals.

Result

Delivered a factory-floor-to-cloud intelligence platform deployed across 3 industrial client sites in cement and energy sectors. The RAG-powered Copilot reduced operator time-to-insight on anomaly alerts, and predictive maintenance workflows enabled equipment teams to act on fault signals before unplanned downtime occurred — shifting site operations from reactive to proactive.→ Add your real metric here when you have it (e.g. "reduced false-alarm rate by 40%")

Personal Project · 2026 · Cairo

NEXUS — Multilingual RAG & NER Intelligence System

LangChainQdrant Gemma4 12BQwen3 Embed GLiNERAraBERT LM StudioPython Re-rankingNER
GitHub (add link)
Situation

Industrial and financial document corpora in the Arab world are inherently mixed-language — the same entity appears as "Al Noor Cement", "Al-Noor Cement Co.", "شركة النور للاسمنت", and "شركة النور" across the same document set. Standard RAG pipelines and off-the-shelf NER models fail here: they can't resolve aliases across scripts, miss Arabic-specific morphology, and hallucinate when the retrieval layer doesn't distinguish person from organization from legal entity.

Task

Build a production-grade multilingual RAG system from scratch — one that indexes thousands of Arabic and English documents, understands entity aliases across both scripts, extracts typed entities (persons, organizations, facilities, legal IDs, contact info) with high precision, and serves grounded answers with source citations — all running locally on a laptop with no cloud dependency.

Action

Architected the full pipeline in LangChain — ingesting PDF, TXT, and MD documents, chunking with overlap-aware strategies, and embedding via Qwen3 Embed (served locally via LM Studio) for dense vector representations stored in Qdrant. Plugged in a re-ranker stage before generation to sharpen retrieval precision on ambiguous cross-language queries.

Built a dual-NER pipeline: GLiNER handles English and European-language documents — extracting persons, organizations, facilities, legal entities, contact details, and IDs with span-level precision. AraBERT (trained on 80 GB of Arabic data) handles Arabic documents — correctly resolving name suffixes, script variants, and morphological forms that Latin-script models miss entirely. Both models run in parallel, surfacing typed entity chips with source citations.

The LLM layer runs Gemma4 12B locally via LM Studio — no API calls, no data leaving the machine — with full system status visible in a live sidebar panel.

Result

Indexed 61,351 vectors across 2,912 files in a single Qdrant collection. Stress-tested on 3,000 files running fully locally. The system correctly resolves entity aliases across Arabic and English scripts — ask in English about a person whose records are in Arabic and it finds them, and vice versa. When asked for a field not present in the documents, the system explicitly cites the source document confirming absence — no hallucination, grounded refusal with evidence.

Academic Project · McMaster University · 2025

Stepper Motor Controller ASIP

Verilog HDLCyclone V FPGA DE1-SoCDatapath / FSM Quartus PrimeSignal Tap
GitHub (add link)
Situation

Stepper motor control in embedded systems typically relies on pre-built microcontroller peripherals. This project required building the control logic from scratch at the hardware level — designing a processor capable of executing a custom instruction set to drive motor motion directly.

Task

Design, implement, and verify a custom ASIP (Application-Specific Instruction-Set Processor) on a DE1-SoC FPGA that controls stepper motor full-step and half-step motion using a purpose-built instruction set and datapath.

Action

Designed the complete Verilog datapath — ALU, register file, program counter, instruction memory, and control unit FSM. Defined a custom instruction set covering motor step direction, delay tracking, and position control. Implemented both full-step and half-step sequencing logic. Verified register-file behavior and instruction execution states using Signal Tap logic analyzer on the Cyclone V FPGA.

Result

A fully functioning ASIP executing custom motor-control instructions on hardware — from architectural design to silicon-level verification — demonstrating end-to-end digital system design at the register-transfer level.

Academic Project · McMaster University · 2025

Ball-and-Beam PID Control System

MATLABSimulink QUARCNI myRIO PID / PD ControlRoot Locus
GitHub (add link)
Situation

The ball-and-beam system is a classic unstable control problem — a ball balancing on a beam whose angle is controlled by a motor. Without active feedback, the ball rolls off. The challenge: model the nonlinear electromechanical dynamics accurately enough that a linear controller works on real hardware.

Task

Derive the plant transfer function from first principles, design PD and PID controllers using root-locus methods, implement them in Simulink with QUARC, and validate performance on a physical NI myRIO-controlled rig — measuring settling time, overshoot, disturbance response, and steady-state error.

Action

Modeled the full electromechanical dynamics of the beam actuator and ball position. Derived the open-loop transfer function and plotted root locus to identify stable controller regions. Designed a PD controller first to achieve fast settling, then added integral action to eliminate steady-state error under disturbance. Deployed via QUARC real-time target on NI myRIO and ran live experiments measuring step response, disturbance rejection, and parameter sensitivity.

Result

Achieved stable ball positioning with controlled settling time and minimal overshoot on the physical hardware — validating the complete design loop from mathematical model to real-time controller on embedded hardware.

Academic Project · McMaster University · 2025

SDRAM Controller & SOPC Integration

Nios VPlatform Designer Embedded CAvalon-MM Quartus PrimeDE1-SoC
GitHub (add link)
Situation

Embedded SoC systems frequently need external DRAM for large data buffers, but integrating a custom memory controller into a processor system requires precise bus protocol compliance. This project required designing a controller that correctly speaks the Avalon-MM bus protocol while satisfying SDRAM timing requirements.

Task

Design and integrate a custom SDRAM controller as an Avalon-MM slave peripheral in a Nios V SOPC system built with Platform Designer, then validate correct 64 MB memory access from embedded C software.

Action

Configured the Nios V SOPC system in Platform Designer — adding the custom SDRAM controller as an Avalon-MM slave with correct address mapping, waitstates, and burst parameters. Wrote embedded C validation firmware exercising byte, short (16-bit), and integer (32-bit) read/write patterns across the full 64 MB address space. Debugged bus timing mismatches using Signal Tap captures.

Result

Successful 64 MB external DRAM access from a Nios V processor — all access widths validated without errors — demonstrating deep understanding of embedded memory architecture and SoC bus protocol integration.

Academic Projects · McMaster University · 2024–2025

Embedded Systems Suite — STM32

STM32Embedded C PWMADC EEPROMRTC Ultrasonic SensorsTouchscreen LCD DC / Stepper Motors
GitHub (add link)
Overview

Six bare-metal STM32 firmware projects, each targeting a different peripheral subsystem. No RTOS — every project runs at the register level with full manual control of timers, interrupts, DMA, and peripherals.

RTC + EEPROM Data Logger

Timestamped sensor readings written to I²C EEPROM using a real-time clock — persisted across power cycles, readable on boot. Tested read/write endurance across address ranges.

Smart PWM Fan Controller

ADC-sampled temperature sensor driving PWM fan speed with hysteresis control — smooth speed ramping without oscillation around threshold temperatures.

Ultrasonic Garage-Door Automation

HC-SR04 ultrasonic ranging triggering motor-driven door open/close with configurable distance thresholds and debounce logic — interrupt-driven, no polling.

Touchscreen LCD Games

Tic-Tac-Toe and reaction-time trainer on a resistive touchscreen LCD — custom graphics rendering, touch coordinate mapping, and game state machines.

PID Line-Following Robot

IR sensor array feeding a PID controller driving differential motor speed — tuned for stable line tracking at speed with minimal oscillation.
